name: Rice-Flour Dosas with Mustard Seeds and Black
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, blend together white flour, rice flour, cayenne pepper, onion, coconut, salt, yogurt, and 3/4 cup water until smooth. Transfer the mixture to a bowl.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a small skillet over medium heat and cook mustard seeds until they begin to pop. Add the seeds and oil, along with black peppercorns, to the batter; stir to combine thoroughly.
3. Have all cooking utensils ready: near your skillet, have at hand a cup or bowl with 1/4 to 1/2 cup vegetable oil, a larger spoon for spreading the batter, a measuring scoop/cup, a metal spatula for turning the pancakes, and a plate for placing finished dosas.
4. Pour about 1 teaspoon of oil into the skillet and tilt to distribute evenly. Heat the skillet over medium-low until the oil is hot.
5. Pour 1/3 cup of the batter onto the center of the hot skillet. Use the larger spoon to spread the batter in a spiral motion, until the pancake is about 6-7 inches in diameter. Turn the pancake after 2 minutes and cook another 2 minutes on the other side.
6. Remove the pancake to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter, adding only enough additional oil to keep the skillet's surface evenly greased (about 1/2 to 3/4 teaspoon per pancake).
7. Serve immediately or reheat wrapped in aluminum foil at 400°F for 15 minutes.
8. Serve with relishes, pickles, chutney, or cooked vegetables.
